hook.text: Scientists estimate that less than half of Earth's caves have been discovered.

buildup.text: Remote jungles, deep oceans, and polar ice sheets hide cave systems yet to be mapped.

discovery.text: New caves with unique species are found every year—even in well-explored countries.

twist.text: Climate change is melting ice and exposing cave entrances sealed for millennia.

climax.text: Beneath the surface, an entire world of geology, biology, and water remains mostly unknown.

punchline.text: We've mapped Mars better than the world beneath our feet.

quiz.question: How much of Earth's cave systems have been discovered?
